_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how authors vary sentence structures for effect — such as using sentence fragments, or deliberately placing dependent clauses in unusual positions.

_source.w: We are learning how authors vary sentence structures for effect — such as using sentence fragments, or deliberately placing dependent clauses in unusual positions.
y: Intentional variation of sentence structure is a sophisticated technique that creates emphasis, rhythm and style.
Understanding these effects helps us analyse how skilled writers craft their prose.
Experimenting with sentence structure is one of the most powerful tools for developing our own writing style.
eg: We analyse how a sentence fragment creates emphasis — 'Gone. Everything was gone.'
We examine how front-loading a dependent clause creates a different effect from placing it at the end.
We compare texts that use varied sentence structures with those that do not and discuss the impact.
We experiment with sentence fragments, single-word sentences and unusual clause placement in our own writing.
We identify how an author uses sentence length variation to control pace — short for tension, long for reflection.
We evaluate when unconventional sentence structures enhance writing versus when they distract.
